rice.overview: Rice is India's staple cereal and a high-water crop suited to humid conditions.
rice.soilPreference: Clay loam to loam with good puddling ability, pH 5.5-7.0

wheat.overview: Major rabi cereal requiring cool growth period and dry maturity phase.
wheat.soilPreference: Well-drained loam to clay loam, pH 6.5-7.5

maize.overview: Versatile cereal grown in kharif, rabi and spring windows based on irrigation.

soybean.overview: Important oilseed and protein crop for monsoon season with moderate water demand.

mustard.overview: Cool-season oilseed crop known for good returns in dry rabi conditions.
mustard.soilPreference: Well-drained loam to sandy loam, pH 6.0-7.5

millet.overview: Drought-tolerant coarse cereal suitable for low-rainfall regions.
millet.soilPreference: Light to medium soils with good drainage, pH 6.0-7.5

chickpea.overview: Major rabi pulse that improves soil fertility through biological nitrogen fixation.
chickpea.soilPreference: Well-drained loam to clay loam, pH 6.0-8.0

cotton.overview: Cash fiber crop requiring warm climate and balanced irrigation through long duration.
cotton.soilPreference: Deep black soils with good moisture retention, pH 6.0-8.0

lentil.overview: Rabi pulse suited to cool weather and low to moderate irrigation.
lentil.soilPreference: Loam to clay loam soils, pH 6.0-7.5

jute.overview: Monsoon fiber crop thriving in warm, humid alluvial plains.
jute.soilPreference: Alluvial loam with good drainage, pH 6.0-7.5

mungbean.overview: Short-duration pulse suitable for multiple windows and catch cropping.

blackgram.overview: Pulse crop grown in kharif and summer, valued for dal and soil health.
blackgram.soilPreference: Loam to clay loam with drainage, pH 6.0-7.5

pigeonpeas.overview: Long-duration pulse with strong drought resilience and deep root system.
pigeonpeas.soilPreference: Well-drained medium to deep soils, pH 6.0-8.0

mothbeans.overview: Arid pulse crop adapted to low rainfall and poor soils.

kidneybeans.overview: Cool-season pulse grown in hills and temperate belts with high market value.

banana.overview: Perennial tropical fruit crop with year-round management and high biomass demand.
banana.soilPreference: Deep fertile loam with drainage, pH 6.0-7.5

mango.overview: Flagship Indian fruit tree requiring long-term orchard planning and canopy care.

grapes.overview: High-value vineyard crop requiring precise irrigation, pruning and disease management.

apple.overview: Temperate fruit crop in hill ecosystems requiring chilling hours and strict canopy management.
apple.soilPreference: Well-drained loam rich in organic matter, pH 5.5-6.8

orange.overview: Commercial citrus crop with regulated irrigation and nutrient programs for fruit quality.
intercroppingOptions: Citrus + short vegetables in early years

papaya.overview: Fast-bearing tropical fruit crop with continuous harvesting under proper nutrient-water balance.

coconut.overview: Perennial coastal and tropical plantation crop with long economic life.
coconut.soilPreference: Well-drained coastal alluvial and lateritic soils, pH 5.2-8.0

coffee.overview: Shade-loving plantation crop grown in humid hill tracts with careful blossom management.
coffee.soilPreference: Well-drained acidic loam rich in organic matter, pH 5.5-6.5

pomegranate.overview: Drought-tolerant fruit crop with high export potential under managed bahar scheduling.
pomegranate.soilPreference: Well-drained light to medium soils, pH 6.0-7.5

watermelon.overview: Warm-season cucurbit crop with high market turnover and strong irrigation dependence.
watermelon.soilPreference: Sandy loam with high drainage, pH 6.0-7.0

muskmelon.overview: Short-duration summer cucurbit valued for sweetness and quick market realization.
muskmelon.soilPreference: Light sandy loam with excellent drainage, pH 6.0-7.0
